title: Salmon with Creamy Spinach Sauce

Seasonings: 1 teaspoon table salt or fine Himalayan pink salt, divided
½ teaspoon freshly ground black pepper, divided
For the Herb Topping: ¼ cup loosely packed fresh basil leaves, chopped
¼ cup loosely packed fresh flat-leaf parsley leaves, chopped
2 teaspoons grated lemon zest plus 1 tablespoon fresh juice (from 1 lemon)

steps: Preheat oven to 425°F with the rack in the top third position. Season the salmon with ¾ teaspoon of the salt and ¼ teaspoon of the pepper; set aside.

Heat olive oil and 1 tablespoon of the butter in a large, high-sided ovenproof skillet over medium-high. Add shallot and garlic; cook, stirring often, until garlic is very fragrant and shallots soften, about 2 minutes. Season with crushed red pepper.

Reduce heat to medium, and stir in cream and mustard. Bring to a simmer over medium; cook, stirring constantly, until sauce thickens slightly, 1 to 2 minutes. Stir in the remaining ¼ teaspoon each of salt and pepper. Add spinach and Parmesan; cook, stirring constantly, until spinach is completely wilted, 1 to 2 minutes. Remove pan from heat, and slide salmon into sauce. Top with the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter.

Bake in the preheated oven until salmon is warm in the center and turning opaque, about 8 minutes or longer to your liking. Increase oven temperature to broil (do not remove skillet from oven). Broil until the top is browned in spots, about 2 minutes.
While salmon cooks, toss basil, parsley, lemon zest, juice, and thyme in a small bowl. Spoon the cream sauce over the salmon. Top with the herb mixture and sprinkle with flaky salt.
